topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay spread across the kitchen table, a stark white sheet against the worn wood. My hand trembled slightly as I traced the lines of the genetic report. It was a document I’d been told would be routine, a formality. But the results… they were a seismic shift in my understanding of myself. I stared at the percentages, the shared markers, the undeniable biological connection that suddenly explained so much I hadn't known. A slow, heavy weight settled in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I picked up the phone, my fingers fumbling over the keypad, the silence in the room amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

"So, what does this all *mean*?" I asked, the words sounding thin and reedy in the quiet of the living room. I kept glancing at the screen, the complex data swimming before my eyes. Every line seemed to pull at something fundamental, a piece of the puzzle I hadn't realized was missing. My jaw was tight, my shoulders hunched as if bracing for an impact. I leaned back against the sofa, the springs groaning under my weight, a physical manifestation of the sheer unexpectedness of the revelation. I kept re-reading the section detailing the shared ancestry, my eyes darting back and forth, searching for some anchor in the sea of unfamiliar information.

[story 3]

The lab technician had been so calm, so matter-of-fact when he handed me the envelope. Now, sitting alone in my room, the silence was deafening. I kept running my thumb over the glossy paper, feeling the texture, trying to ground myself in something tangible. My breathing hitched, shallow and quick. It felt like someone had just rearranged the furniture in my mind, throwing everything out of its expected place. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between the bed and the window, my movements jerky and uncertain. I stopped by the glass, looking out at the ordinary street, utterly disconnected from the reality I now possessed.
